key_insight: XCT-SAM improves industrial XCT defect segmentation by adapting SAM in two stages, using an intermediate alloy-microstructure domain to bridge the gap between natural images and XCT scans.
engineering_relevance: The method offers a practical, parameter-efficient path to deploy foundation models for additive manufacturing quality control with limited labeled XCT data.
method: Fine-tune SAM with Conv-LoRA adapters on an alloy-microstructure dataset first.
Transfer the adapted model to XCT images for sequential domain adaptation.
Use rank r=2 Conv-LoRA to add convolutional spatial bias while training ~4.15M parameters and freezing >99% of the model.
evidence_signal: The model was evaluated on out-of-distribution CycleGAN-XCT benchmarks and real NIST XCT scans, where it outperformed zero-shot SAM and other adapted baselines in IoU and Dice.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality and relevance of the intermediate microstructure dataset, so transfer gains could be weaker if that bridge domain is unavailable or poorly matched.
paper_title: XCT-SAM: Sequential Parameter-Efficient Domain Adaptation of SAM for Industrial XCT Defect Segmentation
